Oracle Events
OE 1, Road Runner vehicle delay email
→ Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "Road Runner" / "vehicle shipment" or similar)
Discover: email_email_a3ca1b6dd238 - dispatch@roadrunnerautotransport.com → blessing.okafor@moveops.com; subject Marcus Webb 2019 Honda Civic → Boston; Civic LX blue at Indianapolis hub, driver shortage; original ETA Apr 8, revised Apr 18–20; RR-482991-BOS; is_read: false.
Key: Links delay to Marcus Webb (BrightLoop, Atlanta → Boston).
OE 2, Marcus Webb follow-ups + Julian / Blessing / Chloe thread
→ Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "Marcus Webb" / "car delivery" / "Julian" or similar)
Discover: Marcus → support@moveops.com (no firm ETA/location); Julian → Marcus: apology, Indy hub, fresh Road Runner pull, update by 2:00 PM PT; Blessing → Chloe (CC Julian, Mina): missed Apr 11 Road Runner notice; Chloe: who owns Marcus update + Road Runner ETA.
Key: Julian did customer recovery; no email confirms delivery - status unconfirmed as of Apr 26, 2026.
OE 3, Airtable - Preethi Nair relocation
→ Read/lookup
Tool: airtable_search_records (non-default) - base_id appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01 (Relocations), field Name, value Preethi Nair
Discover: recReloc00000000036 - BrightLoop, In Progress, Atlanta → Raleigh, dates 2026-04-21–23, coordinator Blessing; Special Requirements: studio/1BR cat, economy flight, $1,800 stipend.
Key: Stale vs email - (1) requirements should be 2BR furnished, spouse, family-friendly, morning flight, $1,800; (2) dates should align Aug 22 per brightloop_req_001 + Mina confirmation (not April).
Prompt bridge: "Update anything outdated" in Airtable - for Blessing-linked BrightLoop rows in this universe, only Preethi Nair (recReloc00000000036) needs field corrections; Marcus Webb row reflects vehicle/Road Runner context without the same date/requirements mismatch called out in email truth.
OE 4, Email - authoritative Preethi / BrightLoop details
→ Read/lookup
Tool: search_emails (query: "Preethi Nair" / "BrightLoop relocation" or similar)
Discover: Tessa -> Mina thread - brightloop_req_001: both moves 2026-08-22; brightloop_log_001: Preethi 2BR furnished, spouse, family-friendly, $1,800, economy morning flight; Mina: Blessing coordinator, options in flight, still Aug 22.
Key: Confirms OE 3 staleness on requirements + move dates.
OE 5, Airtable - all BrightLoop relocations
→ Read/lookup
Tool: airtable_search_records - Company = BrightLoop Analytics
Discover: recReloc00000008 Jordan Ekwueme SD→Boston Completed Suki; recReloc00000000035 Jordan Whitfield MSP→Denver In Progress Javier; recReloc00000000036 Preethi ATL→Raleigh In Progress Blessing; recSimoneRichterBrightloop Simone CHI→Boston In Progress Suki; recMarcusWebbBrightloop Marcus ATL→Boston In Progress Suki + Road Runner vehicle.
Key: Blessing tied to Preethi (coordinator) + Marcus (missed Road Runner); other BrightLoop moves other coordinators.
OE 6, Slack - BrightLoop / vehicle / capacity
→ Read/lookup
Tool: conversations_search_messages (search_query: "BrightLoop" / "Road Runner" / "vehicle" or similar)
Discover: C002 Mina: Whitfield + Nair, Suki/Blessing; C006 Chloe capacity: Blessing - Canopy (Kevin Okafor), BrightLoop (Preethi); #executive Chloe on Road Runner miss; Mina escalation - Tessa 6 May moves, April batch (Simone apt, Marcus vehicle) open.
Key: Account load growing - six May relocations on top of unresolved April issues.
OE 7, Linear - vehicle / BrightLoop tickets
→ Read/lookup
Tool: linear_list_issues (non-default) - query BrightLoop / vehicle / Road Runner / Marcus Webb or similar
Discover: linear_issue_c16357d188c6 P1 Mina team_operations - audit before May expansion, RR delay in scope; linear_issue_f85be674c9b8 P2 Chloe team_operations - ops gaps incl. Marcus / unread RR.
Key: No dedicated vehicle-delivery ticket - conditional: create focused issue (audit tickets ≠ vehicle tracker per prompt).
OE 8, CRM - BrightLoop company + deals
→ Read/lookup
Tool: crm_search_companies (non-default) - name BrightLoop or similar
Discover: company_brightloop; deal_brightloop_apr2026 (Apr 2026 relocations Simone + Marcus, $11,350, closedwon); deal_brightloop_q2 ($7,000, Q2 relocation). Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto.
Key: Active, growing account - context for Mina email + #operations post.
OE 9, Airtable update - Preethi Nair
→ WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: airtable_update_records - base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, record recReloc00000000036 - set Special Requirements to email truth (2BR furnished, spouse, family-friendly, morning economy, $1,800 or similar); Move Start/End to Aug 2026 window (e.g. 2026-08-22 / 2026-08-24 or similar).
Action: Clears stale requirements + April dates from OE 3–4.
OE 10, Linear - dedicated vehicle tracking issue
→ WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: linear_create_issue - team team_operations or team_relocations; title/description: Marcus Webb + Road Runner delivery, Indy hub, Julian recovery, delivery unconfirmed, follow-ups; priority P2+.
Action: New ticket separate from linear_issue_c16357d188c6 + linear_issue_f85be674c9b8.
OE 11, Email - Mina (Preethi + BrightLoop open items)
→ WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: send_email - sender blessing.okafor@moveops.com, to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com; subject Preethi / BrightLoop (or similar); body: Preethi ATL→Raleigh status, Airtable fix note, ask open BrightLoop items, vehicle delay status.
Action: Meets prompt to Mina.
OE 12, Slack - #operations status
→ WRITE (Outcome rubrics only)
Tool: conversations_add_message - channel_id C006 (#operations); payload: Marcus/Road Runner (unconfirmed, Julian, new ticket), Preethi status, Airtable correction, Blessing BrightLoop picture for capacity review.
Action: Meets prompt to Chloe.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record) to look up relocation records
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent used airtable_search_records multiple times: querying by Company='BrightLoop', Name='Preethi Nair', Name='Marcus Webb', and Assigned Coordinator='Blessing Okafor'. It also called airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables.
✓Run #2The agent called airtable_list_bases, airtable_list_tables, and airtable_search_records (with Company=BrightLoop) to look up relocation records. Multiple Airtable read calls are confirmed in the trajectory.
✓Run #3The agent called airtable_search_records twice (once for 'Relocations' table with Company='BrightLoop' and once for 'Client Accounts' table with Client='BrightLoop'). Airtable read tools were clearly used to look up relocation records.
✓Run #4The agent called airtable_search_records multiple times: searching Relocations by Company='BrightLoop', by Name='Preethi Nair', and by Name='Marcus Webb'. Also called airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables to discover the schema. Clearly passes.
✓Run #5The agent used airtable_search_records multiple times: searching by Company='BrightLoop', by Name='Preethi Nair', by Name='Marcus Webb', and by Employee Name in Stipend Transactions. Also used airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables. Clear Airtable read tool usage throughout the trajectory.
✓Run #6The agent used airtable_search_records multiple times: searching Relocations table by Company='BrightLoop', by Name='Marcus Webb', by Employee Name='Preethi Nair' in Stipend Transactions, and searching Client Accounts by Client='BrightLoop'. Airtable read tools were clearly used.
2 The model must use a Linear read tool (e.g., linear_list_issues or linear_get_issue) to check for existing BrightLoop or vehicle-related tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues with queries 'Road Runner vehicle', 'BrightLoop', 'vehicle', and 'Marcus Webb Road Runner' before calling linear_create_issue. This confirms it checked for existing tickets first.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times before creating the new vehicle issue — with queries 'Road Runner vehicle', 'vehicle', 'Marcus Webb', 'BrightLoop', and 'BrightLoop audit'. All returned results or empty arrays, confirming the read step preceded the create.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'vehicle', then again with 'BrightLoop', and again with 'Road Runner' before creating the new ticket. This confirms a Linear read was performed to check for existing tickets.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_list_issues with queries 'Road Runner vehicle', 'vehicle', 'BrightLoop', and 'Marcus Webb' before creating any ticket. All returned empty, confirming no existing ticket. The read was performed before the create_issue call.
✓Run #5The agent used linear_list_issues three times before creating a ticket: querying 'Road Runner vehicle' (empty), 'vehicle' (empty), and 'BrightLoop' (returned existing issues). This verifies no dedicated vehicle-delivery ticket existed prior to creation.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times before creating the ticket: querying 'Road Runner vehicle', 'vehicle', 'Marcus Webb', and 'BrightLoop'. All returned empty results, confirming no existing ticket, which then justified the creation.
3 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_companies, crm_search_deals, or crm_get_company) to look up BrightLoop account context.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_search_companies with name='BrightLoop' and subsequently crm_search_deals with company_id='company_brightloop', successfully retrieving BrightLoop account context.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_search_companies with name='BrightLoop' and received BrightLoop Analytics company data including contact IDs and account details.
✓Run #3The agent called crm_search_companies with name='BrightLoop', which returned BrightLoop Analytics company record including account details.
✓Run #4The agent called crm_search_companies with name='BrightLoop' and received results for 'BrightLoop Analytics' including company details. The CRM was queried for account context.
✓Run #5The agent used crm_search_companies with name='BrightLoop' and received BrightLoop Analytics data. Also called crm_list_engagements with company_ids=['company_brightloop'], returning engagement context including Mina Hashimoto and deal details.
✓Run #6The agent used crm_search_companies with name='BrightLoop' which returned BrightLoop Analytics company record, and also called crm_search_deals with company_id='company_brightloop' to look up account deals.
4 The Airtable record for Preethi Nair (recReloc00000000036) was updated (via airtable_update_records or equivalent) and the tool response indicates success.
